hypothesis: There exists an optimal intermediate branching degree—approximately 30–50% of monomer units acting as branch points—in amine-based polymer sorbents such that, under DAC-relevant conditions (298 K, CO2 partial pressure 0.4 mbar) and humid environments (2–3 wt% H2O), polymers with this branching degree achieve ≥15% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake (measured by long-timescale MD-relaxed GCMC) than otherwise identical linear or highly crosslinked polymers.
